nativeRange.habitat: Reef-associated; inhabits holes and crevices in rocky and coral reef substrates

diet.notes: Has been documented feeding mainly on crabs and fish in the wild. In captivity tends to accept fresh or frozen meaty foods such as silversides, shrimp, and squid; live food may be used to entice newly acquired specimens.

summary: A temperate SW Pacific moray eel reaching around 90 cm, commonly traded as the Green Eel. Generally considered suitable for intermediate-level marine keepers who can provide cool, well-oxygenated water and substantial rockwork.
careNotes:
Gymnothorax prasinus is a temperate species native to New Zealand, the Kermadec Islands, and southern Australia, and is reported by FishBase to prefer water temperatures between roughly 16–24°C — cooler than most tropical marine aquarium species. Aquarists keeping this species may need a chiller to maintain appropriate temperature. The species has been reported to stay in holes and crevices during the day and tend to be most active at night (nocturnal), so feeding in the evening is generally advised. Like most moray eels, it has been known to be an escape artist and a tightly sealed, weighted lid is strongly recommended. The species tends to be aggressive toward smaller tankmates and small invertebrates. It is generally considered reef-unsafe with crustaceans (crabs, shrimp) as these form a natural part of its diet. Note: this species is sometimes sold as 'Yellow Moray' and should not be confused with the much larger Green Moray (Gymnothorax funebris), which can exceed 2.4 m and requires vastly larger systems. Copper-based medications should be avoided as morays lack true scales and tend to be sensitive to copper treatments.

RESOLVED 2026-06-03: The flagged concern (LiveAquaria /products/green-eel URL serving G. funebris data instead of G. prasinus) is confirmed but does not affect data integrity here. FishBase independently verifies all G. prasinus parameters used in this record: max size 91.5 cm TL, temperature 15.7–23.6°C, native range SW Pacific (New Zealand coast from North Cape to Mahia Peninsula, Kermadec Islands, southern Australia), depth 0–40 m, diet mainly crabs and fish, IUCN Least Concern. FishBase (G. funebris) confirms the two species are completely distinct: G. funebris is a Western Atlantic species reaching 250 cm, temperature 23–28°C — no overlap in range, size, or thermal preference. Wikipedia (stub) corroborates the NZ/southern Australia range for G. prasinus. The LiveAquaria catalog error does not contaminate any field in this record; all data sourced from FishBase directly. depthRangeM updated from null to 0–40 m using FishBase data.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Green Eel listed at $198.99; care level Moderate, Reef Compatible With Caution. The /products/green-eel detail URL returned data for Gymnothorax funebris (the large Green Moray), not G. prasinus — a possible catalog error. Product listing attributes (care level, reef compatibility) were used; sizing and range data deferred to FishBase.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Primary data source for confirmed scientific identification, max size (91.5 cm TL), native range (New Zealand, Kermadec Islands, southern Australia), temperature range (15.7–23.6°C mean 18°C), depth range (0–40 m), diet (mainly crabs and fish), habitat (reef-associated, nocturnal, stays in crevices), IUCN Least Concern. Species ID 10146. Also cross-checked G. funebris entry (250 cm, 23–28°C, W. Atlantic) confirming no data overlap with G. prasinus. BlueZoo Aquatics SSL certificate expired.

additional.name: Wikipedia
additional.notes: Stub article. Corroborates native range: southern Australia and North Island New Zealand coast (North Cape to Mahia Peninsula). No additional quantitative data beyond FishBase.
